lesson.menu_title: Around Town -- Invitations and Suggestions (would you like / let's)
lesson.menu_desc: Invite a friend and make plans: Would you like to go to the cinema? Let's have dinner together. New words: cinema, concert, theater, accept, refuse, busy, sounds good, join -- 28 slides
lesson.subtitle: Aula 17 -- Around Town: Invitations and Suggestions (would you like / let's)
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Érica Maria Machado Santarem | Aula 17 -- Around Town: Invitations and Suggestions

listenings.text: Hello. I want to invite my friend tonight. Listen to me. I am going to call her. I will say: Hi! Would you like to go to the cinema with me? There is a nice movie tonight. Maybe she is busy. Maybe she will say: Sorry, I am busy tonight. But maybe she will say: Yes! That sounds good. I would love to join you. I hope she can come. It is more fun together. Let us see what she says.

Hi. Let me tell you about my evening. My colleague invited me to a concert. He said: Would you like to come to a concert on Friday? I was very happy. I said: Yes, that sounds good. I would love to join you. But then I remembered. On Friday I am busy. So I had to refuse. I said: Sorry, I am busy on Friday. Maybe next time. He understood. Next week we are going to go to the theater together. I will not refuse that one.

extra_audio.text: First, my friend asks: Would you like to go to the cinema tonight? Second, I answer: Yes, that sounds good. Third, my friend says: Great. Let us meet at seven. Fourth, I ask: Can my sister join us? Fifth, my friend says: Of course. The more, the better.
